title: MCAD Complaints of Discrimination | Mass.gov
content:
# MCAD Complaints of Discrimination | Mass.gov

If you believe you experienced unfair treatment based on your membership in a protected class, you may file a complaint of discrimination with the MCAD.

The MCAD enforces Massachusetts anti-discrimination laws by investigating complaints that allege discrimination in employment, housing, public accommodations, higher education admissions, credit, and lending as a neutral entity. If it is found that discrimination has likely occurred, the MCAD prosecutes and adjudicates such cases.

## What Would You Like to Do?

- File a Complaint of Discrimination at the MCAD
- Request an MCAD Case Status Update
- File a Motion to Amend, Appeal, Remove, or Withdraw an MCAD Case
- Respond to an MCAD Complaint
- Overview of Anti-Discrimination Laws Enforced by the MCAD
- Guide to the MCAD Case Process

## Frequently Asked Questions

### How long do I have to file a complaint of discrimination with the MCAD?

In most cases, you have 300 days from the last discriminatory act to file a complaint (6 months for higher education admission complaints).

### What if I am an out-of-state attorney?

Out-of-state attorneys may practice before the MCAD with certain requirements.
